title: Heatmap Regression for Keypoints
concept: Neural networks detect keypoint locations by predicting heatmaps containing 2D Gaussian distributions centered on the joint coordinates.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is the main drawback of top-down pose estimation?

options.text: Its inference time increases with the number of people in the image.
It is less accurate than bottom-up.
It cannot detect knees or ankles.

exercises.explain: Correct! Because it detects and processes each person sequentially, top-down models slow down as the crowd size increases.

cards.title: Coordinates are hard
cards.body: If you ask a neural network to directly output raw (x,y) numbers for a joint (e.g. `Knee: [243.5, 512.2]`), the network struggles. Deep CNNs are great at spatial features, but poor at outputting raw numbers. To make it easy, we transform the target into a picture.

cards.title: What is Heatmap Regression?
cards.body: Instead of predicting coordinates directly, networks predict a heatmap for each keypoint. A heatmap is a grid (like a tiny grayscale image) where the pixel values represent probability. We place a 2D Gaussian distribution (a smooth peak) centered on the joint's true location.

cards.title: Weather radar
cards.body: Think of a heatmap like a weather radar map showing rain. The radar doesn't output a single coordinate of 'the storm center'. It shows a colorful blob that is red-hot in the middle and fades out. The peak of that temperature corresponds to the center of the joint.

cards.title: Hourglass Networks
cards.body: Architectures like the Stacked Hourglass Network take an image and output a stack of heatmaps (e.g., one heatmap for the left shoulder, one for the right knee). The post-processing step simply finds the pixel index with the **maximum value** (the peak) to get the final coordinate.

cards.title: Not just direct regression
cards.body: A common myth is that models always predict joint coordinates directly. In reality, training a model to predict heatmaps is much more stable and accurate, as it allows convolutional layers to preserve spatial context rather than collapsing features into raw coordinates.

cards.title: Direct Output vs. Heatmaps
cards.body: Comparing target formats for keypoint training:

left.text:
Outputs raw decimals
Hard for CNNs to optimize
Unstable predictions

right.text:
Outputs 2D grid image
Smooth Gaussian peak at joint
Highly stable, standard choice

exercises.prompt: How does a heatmap regression model represent the location of a joint in training?

options.text: As a 2D Gaussian distribution peak centered on the joint coordinate
As a single bounding box around the whole limb
As an ASCII character representing the joint name

exercises.explain: In heatmap regression, targets are represented as 2D Gaussian probability distributions on a grid.

exercises.prompt: True or False: To extract the final (x,y) coordinates from a predicted heatmap, we find the location of the maximum probability value (the peak).
exercises.explain: True. Finding the maximum value (argmax) in the predicted heatmap grid gives the localized keypoint coordinates.

exercises.sentence: Predicting a probability grid for joints is called heatmap ___.
exercises.explain: Heatmap regression is the standard term for training networks to output spatial probability maps of keypoints.

exercises.prompt: Which description represents heatmap regression output?

options.text: A 64x64 grid where pixel intensities fade out from a bright spot at the elbow location
A crop box showing only the elbow image

exercises.explain: A grid containing a fading intensity peak is a visual description of a heatmap.

exercises.prompt: You are training a model to detect hand knuckles. The model outputs joint coordinates that jump around wildly between consecutive video frames. What adjustment should you make to the training targets?

options.text: Replace the direct coordinate output with heatmap regression using a tight Gaussian peak centered on each knuckle.
Remove the convolutional layers and use a single dense layer to output raw coordinates.
Increase the learning rate to make the outputs jump faster.
Convert the hand photos to 1-pixel resolution images.

exercises.explain: Exactly! Heatmaps preserve spatial gradients, helping the network learn smooth, stable spatial representations and avoiding the instabilities of direct coordinate regression. Outstanding! Next: connecting joints into bodies.
